response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced generalist with strong web, full-stack, and systems credentials, but the role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise for industrial automation. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a university thesis project, with the bulk of his career in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He shows no experience with MQTT, message brokers in a C++ context, IEC 62443, or industrial secure-development practices, making him a weak match despite his seniority.
strengths: Extensive overall engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: Very limited professional C++ experience; core stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, not C++/STL
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, or MQTT/OT-protocol experience
No demonstrated Azure or message-broker-based C++ architecture work required by the role
